notes.content: The Genetics & Genomics major provides a broad background in the biological, mathematical, and physical sciences basic to the study of heredity, gene expression and evolution. The major is sufficiently flexible to accommodate students interested in the subject either as a basic discipline in the biological sciences or in terms of its applied aspects such as biotechnology, medicine, and agriculture. The upper division curriculum in the Genetics & Genomics program begins with the four-course, upper-division core curriculum that provides an introduction to the principles of genetics, biochemistry, and cell biology. Students then take additional upper-division courses in specialized areas of modern genetics including gene expression, evolution, development, human genetics, and genomics, as well as laboratory courses in the principles of genetics and genomics. Additional upper-division courses in biological sciences, as well as internship/research coursework, can be chosen to fulfill required elective units.
notes.contentHtml:
The Genetics & Genomics major provides a broad background in the biological, mathematical, and physical sciences basic to the study of heredity, gene expression and evolution. The major is sufficiently flexible to accommodate students interested in the subject either as a basic discipline in the biological sciences or in terms of its applied aspects such as biotechnology, medicine, and agriculture.


The upper division curriculum in the Genetics & Genomics program begins with the four-course, upper-division core curriculum that provides an introduction to the principles of genetics, biochemistry, and cell biology. Students then take additional upper-division courses in specialized areas of modern genetics including gene expression, evolution, development, human genetics, and genomics, as well as laboratory courses in the principles of genetics and genomics. Additional upper-division courses in biological sciences, as well as internship/research coursework, can be chosen to fulfill required elective units.

notes.content: The Genetics & Genomics degree provides suitable preparation for a wide variety of careers, including teaching, research, work with biotechnology companies, medicine, and all the health sciences. It is also an excellent background for students wishing to continue their education in a graduate program, a teacher-training program, medical school, veterinary school, or other professional schools.
notes.contentHtml: The Genetics & Genomics degree provides suitable preparation for a wide variety of careers, including teaching, research, work with biotechnology companies, medicine, and all the health sciences. It is also an excellent background for students wishing to continue their education in a graduate program, a teacher-training program, medical school, veterinary school, or other professional schools.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission. Important Information: You must complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series: ● Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C ● Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C ● Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission) ● Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C ● Physics 7A/B/C Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.

 

Important Information:


You must complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series:

●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C

●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C

●     Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B


Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission)


●     Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C

●     Physics 7A/B/C


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

notes.content: Group 2, Group 3 , Group 4 and Group 5: Please note that students may be able to meet major requirements with different combinations of courses. Please visit the major for more details and advising.
